title: dasyphyllum infundibulare
intro: Dasyphyllum infundibulare, also known as the 'cup plant' or 'cupped daisy', is a flowering plant species native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids content. Dasyphyllum infundibulare has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
